# JMS Connector - Full Documentation

# JMS Connector 1.10

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for JMS (Java Message Service) (JMS Connector) enables sending and receiving
messages to queues and topics for any message service that implements the JMS specification. JMS is
a widely used API for message-oriented middleware. It enables the communication between different
components of a distributed application to be loosely coupled, reliable, and asynchronous. The main
features of JMS Connector include:

- Publish and subscribe pattern support on any given destination
- Listen and reply pattern support on any given destination
- Publish and consume pattern support on any given destination, with a fixed or temporary reply
  queue
- Fully compliant with JMS 2.0, 1.1, and 1.0.2 specifications

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- Java Message Service
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- Creating a Mule app using Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

Before creating an app, you must have access to Anypoint Platform and Anypoint Studio.

## Common Use Cases for the Connector

JMS Connector enables you to:

- Listen for new messages as they arrive at a specified destination.
- Publish a new message and send it to a specified destination.
- Consume a message at any given time in the flow from any given specified destination.
- Publish a message to any specified destination, and then consume a reply on a different specified
  destination.

For examples, see [JMS Connector Examples](jms-examples).

## JMS Messaging Support Models

JMS supports two models for messaging:

- Point-to-point queues
- Publish and subscribe topics

### Point-to-Point Queues

In the point-to-point queuing model, a sender posts messages to a particular queue and a receiver
reads those messages from that same queue. In this model, the sender knows the destination of the
message and posts the message directly to it.

The point-to-point queuing model operates as follows:

- Only one receiver consumes the received message.
- The sender does not need to be running at the time the receiver consumes the message, and the
  receiver does not need to be running at the time the message is sent.
- Every message that is successfully processed is acknowledged by the receiver.

### Publish and Subscribe Topics

The publish and subscribe model supports a sender in publishing messages to a particular message
topic rather than directly to a particular queue. Receivers subscribe to consume the receive
messages on a particular message topic. In this model, neither the sender nor the receiver are aware
of each other. An anonymous bulletin board is a good analogy for this concept.

The publish and subscribe model operates as follows:

- Multiple receivers consume the receive the message, including none if there are no subscribers to
  the topic.
- The sender must create a message topic in such a way that receivers can subscribe to that topic.
- The receiver must remain continuously active to consume the receive messages, unless it has
  established a durable subscription in which case messages sent while the receiver is not connected
  are redistributed when the receiver reconnects.

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites, you are ready to create your own app and configure the
connector using [Anypoint Studio](jms-studio-configuration).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Configure Message Acknowledgement with the JMS Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for JMS (JMS Connector) provides different message acknowledgement modes to
configure for the **Ack**, **Consume**, **On New Message**, and **Recovery session** operations:

- Automatic acknowledgement on success
- Immediate acknowledgement
- Manual acknowledgement

The following examples illustrate how to configure each message acknowledgement mode.

## Configure Automatic Acknowledgement on Success Mode

Configure the automatic acknowledgement on success mode in the **On New Message** source by setting
the **Acknowledge Mode** field to either one of these modes:

- **AUTO**  
  Automatically acknowledges (ACK) the received message only if the flow execution completes
  successfully; otherwise, the message is not acknowledged and is redelivered.
- **DUPS_OK**  
  Like **AUTO** mode, automatically acknowledges (ACK) the received message only if the flow
  execution completes successfully, but leads to duplicate messages if the message is redelivered
  before the ACK is performed.

In the following example, you configure the **AUTO** acknowledgement mode:

1.  In Studio, select the **On New Message** source from your flow.
2.  In the **On New Message** configuration screen, in **Destination**, specify the name of the
    destination from which to consume the message, for example, `#[vars.destination]`.
3.  From the **Acknowledge Mode** menu, select **AUTO**.

![In the On New Message source screen](_images/jms-ack-auto.png)

Figure 1. JMS Connector Auto Acknowledgement Mode configuration

In the XML editor, the `<jms:listener>` and `ackMode` configurations look like this:

```xml
<jms:listener  config-ref="JMS_Config" destination="#[vars.destination]" ackMode="AUTO"/>
```

## Configure Immediate Acknowledgement Mode

Configure the immediate acknowledgement mode in the **On New Message** source by using the
**Acknowledge Mode** field, or in the **Consume** operation by using the **Ack mode** field. Set the
fields to **IMMEDIATE**. This mode automatically acknowledges (ACK) the message after it is consumed
and before to any application processing of the message.  
The result of automatically acknowledging the message is that the message wonât be redelivered if
any error occurs during message processing, requiring application logic like a "dead letter" queue
to manage errors without losing messages.

In the following example, you configure the **IMMEDIATE** acknowledgement mode for the **Consume**
operation:

1.  In Studio, select the **Consume** operation from your flow.
2.  In the **Consume** configuration screen, in **Destination**, specify the name of the destination
    from which to consume the message, for example, `#[vars.destination]`.
3.  From the **Ack mode** menu, select **IMMEDIATE**.

![In the Consume operation screen](_images/jms-ack-immediate.png)

Figure 2. JMS Connector Immediate Acknowledgement Mode configuration

In the XML editor, the `<jms:consume>` and `ackMode` configurations look like this:

## Configure Manual Acknowledgement Mode

Configure the manual acknowledgement mode in the **On New Message** source by using the
**Acknowledge Mode** field, or in the **Consume** operation by using the **Ack mode** field. Set
these fields to **MANUAL**. This mode delegates all responsibility for performing message
acknowledgement (ACK) to the application logic.

Every message received by either the **On New Message** or **Consume** operations includes an
acknowledgement ID in the Mule message attributes, that identifies the message uniquely for a given
connection.  
The acknowledgement ID that identifies the message is then passed to the **Ack** operation in the
**Ack id** field.

In the following example, you configure the **MANUAL** acknowledgement mode for the **Consume**
operation:

1.  In Studio, select the **Consume** operation from your flow.
2.  In the **Consume** configuration screen, in **Destination**, specify the name of the destination
    from which to consume the message, for example, `openTickets`.
3.  From the **Ack mode** menu, select **MANUAL**.
4.  Drag a **Logger** component to the right of the **Consume** operation.
5.  Set the **Message** field to `#[payload]`.
6.  Drag an **Ack** operation to the right of the **Logger** component.
7.  Set the **Ack id** field to the acknowledgement ID of the message session to recover, for
    example, `#[attributes.ackId]`.

![In the Ack operation screen](_images/jms-ack-operation.png)

Figure 3. JMS Connector Ack operation configuration

In the XML editor, the `<jms:consume>`, `ackMode`, and `ackId` configurations look like this:

```xml
<flow name="consumerWithManualAck">
    <jms:consume config-ref="JMS_config" destination="openTickets" ackMode="MANUAL"/>

    <!--Do message processing-->
    <logger message="#[payload]">

    <jms:ack ackId="#[attributes.ackId]"/>
</flow>
```

### Configure Manual Session Recovery

With manual acknowledgement mode, all messages received but not acknowledged are not redelivered by
the broker. If there is an error during the message processing, you must manually recover all the
messages in the session that have to be redelivered by using the **Recover session** operation. The
connection used to recover the session has to be the same as the connection for receiving the
message.

In the following example, you configure the **MANUAL** acknowledgement mode and how to recover the
session:

1.  In Studio, select the **Consume** operation from your flow.
2.  In the **Consume** configuration screen, in **Destination**, specify the name of the destination
    from which to consume the message, for example, `${destination}`.
3.  Set the **Ack mode** field to **MANUAL**.
4.  Drag a **Logger** component to the right of the **Consume** operation.
5.  Set the **Message** field to `#[payload]`.
6.  Drag an **Ack** operation to the right of the **Logger** component.
7.  Set the **Ack id** field to the acknowledgement ID of the message session to recover, for
    example, `#[vars.consumedMessage.attributes.ackId]`.
8.  In your flow, expand the **Error handling** section by clicking the small arrow.
9.  Drag an **On Error Propagate** component in the **Error handling** section.
10. Drag a **Recover session** operation inside the **On Error Propagate** component.
11. Set the **Ack id** field to `#[vars.consumedMessage.attributes.ackId]`.

![In the Recover session operation screen](_images/jms-ack-recover.png)

Figure 4. JMS Connector Recover session operation configuration

In the XML editor, the `<jms:consume>`, `ackMode`, and `ackId` configurations look like this:

```xml
<flow name="consumerWithManualAck">
    <jms:consume config-ref="JMS_config" destination="${destination}"
                 ackMode="MANUAL" target="consumedMessage" targetValue="#[message]"/>

    <!--Do message processing-->
    <logger message="#[payload]">

    <jms:ack ackId="#[vars.consumedMessage.attributes.ackId]"/>

    <error-handler>
        <on-error-continue>
            <!--In case of error, recover the session-->
            <jms:recover-session ackId="#[vars.consumedMessage.attributes.ackId]"/>
        </on-error-continue>
    </error-handler>
</flow>
```

## See Also

- [Configure Topic Subscriptions](jms-topic-subscription)
- [Listen for New Messages](jms-listener)
---

# Configure an ActiveMQ Connection for JMS Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for JMS (JMS Connector) includes an ActiveMQ connection configuration, which
provides support for ActiveMQ 5. Using this connection, you can set JMS 1.1 (default), JMS 1.0.2b,
or JMS 2.0 specifications, and you can configure not only all of the general connection parameters
for JMS but also the custom parameters that are present only in ActiveMQ.

## Configure an ActiveMQ Connection

After you declare the ActiveMQ connection, customize **Connection Factory** to your desired
configuration. Every parameter in the connection comes with a default value, meaning that you are
required to configure only the parameters relevant for your use case. Also, the ActiveMQ connection
exposes parameters that are exclusive to the ActiveMQ implementation, like **Initial redelivery
delay**.

In the following example, you configure an **ActiveMQ Connection**:

1.  In Studio, navigate to the **Global Elements** tab.
2.  Click **Create**.
3.  In the filter box type `jms` and select **JMS Config**.
4.  Click **OK**.
5.  In the **Connection** field select **ActiveMQ Connection**.
6.  In the **Factory configuration** field, select **Edit Inline**.
7.  Set the **Broker url** field value to the address of the broker to connect to, for example,
    `tcp://localhost:61616`.
8.  Click **OK**.

![ActiveMQ Connection Configuration window in Studio with Broker URL field set to tcp://localhost:61616](_images/jms-activemq-configuration.png)

In the XML editor, the `<jms:active-mq-connection>` and `<jms:factory-configuration>` configuration
looks like this:

```xml
 <jms:config name="JMS_Config">
  <jms:active-mq-connection >
   <jms:factory-configuration brokerUrl="tcp://localhost:61616" />
  </jms:active-mq-connection>
 </jms:config>
```

## Configure an ActiveMQ - No Connectivity Test Connection (DEPRECATED)

There may be certain scenarios in which more than one node cannot use the credentials of a broker
connection simultaneously. In these cases, Mule applications use the **Primary node only** field set
to `true` to ensure listener execution on only one of the nodes. However, the connection test that
is performed prior to deployment can result in a deployment error on the primary node if another
node already used the credentials. For this particular case, use **ActiveMQ Connection - No
Connectivity Test**, which is identical to the **ActiveMQ Connection**, except that it does not
perform a connectivity test prior to deployment.

In the following example, you configure an **ActiveMQ Connection - No Connectivity Test**:

1.  In Studio, navigate to the **Global Elements** tab.
2.  Click **Create**.
3.  In the filter box, type `jms` and select **JMS Config**.
4.  Click **OK**.
5.  In the **Connection** field, select **ActiveMQ Connection - No Connectivity Test**.
6.  Set the optional **Client id** field.
7.  In the **Factory configuration** field, select **Edit Inline**.
8.  Set the **Broker url** field value to the address of the broker to connect to, for example,
    `tcp://localhost:61616`.
9.  Click **OK**.

![ActiveMQ Connection - No Connectivity Test Configuration window in Studio with Broker URL field set to tcp://localhost:61616](_images/jms-activemq-nct-configuration.png)

In the XML editor, the `<jms:active-mq-nct-connection>` and `<jms:factory-configuration>`
configuration looks like this:

```xml
 <jms:config name="JMS_Config">
  <jms:active-mq-nct-connection clientId="my-mule-cli">
   <jms:factory-configuration brokerUrl="tcp://localhost:61616" />
  </jms:active-mq-nct-connection>
 </jms:config>
```

## Configure the Required Libraries

When you configure any connection type, you must always configure a library containing the JMS
client implementation, because JMS Connector is not bound to any particular implementation.
Configure ActiveMQ external libraries in the global elements view of Studio or manually add the
library dependency in your `pom.xml` file.

- **ActiveMQ KahaDB**  
  The `activemq-kahadb-store` dependency is required only when using an persistent in-memory broker
  based on the VM transport (such as `vm://localhost?broker.persistent=true`). This dependency
  provides a valid `org.apache.activemq.store.kahadb.KahaDBPersistenceAdapter` implementation. This
  dependency is also required for persistent message delivery.

  In your `pom.xml` file in Studio, adding the dependency looks like this:

  ```xml
  <dependency>
      <groupId>org.apache.activemq</groupId>
      <artifactId>activemq-kahadb-store</artifactId>
      <version>5.14.4</version>
  </dependency>
  ```

- **ActiveMQ Broker**  
  The `activemq-broker` dependency is required only when using an in-memory broker based on the VM
  transport (which is the one configured by default). This dependency provides a valid
  `org.apache.activemq.broker.Broker` implementation.

  In your `pom.xml` file in Studio, adding the dependency looks like this:

  ```xml
  <dependency>
      <groupId>org.apache.activemq</groupId>
      <artifactId>activemq-broker</artifactId>
      <version>5.14.4</version>
  </dependency>
  ```

- **ActiveMQ Client**  
  The `activemq-client` dependency is always required to use ActiveMQ connections. Choose one that
  provides a valid `org.apache.activemq.ActiveMQConnectionFactory` implementation.

In your `pom.xml` file in Studio, adding the dependency looks like this:

-

```xml
<dependency>
     <groupId>org.apache.activemq</groupId>
     <artifactId>activemq-client</artifactId>
     <version>5.14.4</version>
 </dependency>
```

To configure these libraries in the Global Element view, follow these steps:

1.  In Studio, navigate to the **Global Elements** tab.
2.  Click **Create**.
3.  In the filter box, type `jms` and select **JMS Config**.
4.  Click **OK**.
5.  In the **Connection** field, select **ActiveMQ Connection**.
6.  In the **Required Libraries** section that shows the **ActiveMQ KahaDB**, **ActiveMQ Broker**
    and **ActiveMQ Client** libraries, click the **Configureâ¦â** button to install the dependency.
7.  Select any of the following install options:
    - **Add recommended library** Installs the recommended library.
    - **Use local file** Browse to a local file for the required engine library and install it.
    - **Add Maven dependency** Browse to the dependency and install it.

![Click the Configure button to install the dependencies](_images/jms-activemq-libaries.png)

Figure 1. JMS Connector Libraries configuration

## Configure the In-Memory Broker

By default, Anypoint Connector for ActiveMQ (ActiveMQ Connector) uses an in-memory broker, which
makes it easy to start building an application without configuring a connection against an external
broker. The in-memory broker is required to configure both the **ActiveMQ KahaDB** and **ActiveMQ
Broker** libraries. The default URL is:

`vm://localhost?broker.persistent=false&broker.useJmx=false`

To configure the in-memory broker in Studio, follow these steps:

1.  In Studio, open the **JMS Config** global element window.
2.  In the **Connection Factory** section, set the **Factory configuration** field to **Edit
    inline**.
3.  Set the **Broker url** field to the address of the broker to connect, for example,
    `vm://localhost?broker.persistent=false&broker.useJmx=false` .
4.  Click **OK**.

![Set the Broker url field to the address of the broker to connect](_images/jms-activemq-broker.png)

Figure 2. JMS Connector In-Memory Broker Configuration

In the XML editor, the `brokerUrl` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<jms:config name="JMS_Config" doc:name="JMS Config">
		<jms:active-mq-connection >
			<jms:factory-configuration brokerUrl="vm://localhost?broker.persistent=false&amp;broker.useJmx=false"/>
		</jms:active-mq-connection>
	</jms:config>
```

## Configure Message Redelivery

When a message cannot be processed correctly, itâs not acknowledged. The message is then redelivered
and probably again not processed correctly, which causes the cycle to execute indefinitely.

To prevent a message from endless redelivery, configure the **Max redelivery** field.  
By default, JMS Connector uses a maximum redelivery value of `0`, which means that messages wonât be
redelivered, regardless of whether the message is recovered or rolled back from a transaction. If
the message has persistent delivery set, ActiveMQ sends the message to a `DLQ.QUEUE` (a dead letter
queue).

ActiveMQ also enables you to configure a client-side redelivery.

- The **Initial redelivery delay** field enables you to configure, in milliseconds, how much time to
  wait before the first message redelivers.
- The **Redelivery delay** field enables you to configure, in milliseconds, how much time to wait
  until the message is subsequently redelivered after the first redelivery.

To configure these fields in Studio, follow these steps:

1.  In Studio, open the **JMS Config** global element window.
2.  In the **Connection Factory** section, set the **Factory configuration** field to **Edit
    inline**.
3.  Set the **Initial redelivery delay** field to `1000`.
4.  Set the **Redelivery delay** field to `100`.
5.  Set the **Max redelivery** field to `10`.
6.  Click **OK**.

![Set the Max redelivery field to 10](_images/jms-activemq-redelivery.png)

Figure 3. JMS Connector Redelivery configuration

In the XML editor, the `maxRedelivery`,`redeliveryDelay`, and `initialRedeliveryDelay`
configurations look like this:

```xml
<jms:config name="JMS_Config">
  <jms:active-mq-connection>
    <jms:factory-configuration maxRedelivery="10"
      redeliveryDelay="100"
      initialRedeliveryDelay="1000"/>
  </jms:active-mq-connection>
</jms:config>
```

## Configure Trusted Packages

ActiveMQ versions 5.12.2, 5.13.0, and later restrict the classes that can be serialized and
deserialized, helping to prevent the execution of a malicious payload on the host system.

For ActiveMQ, JMS Connector enables by default only the JDK and JRE provided classes, so if you need
to exchange object messages, you must add the packages your applications use by activating the
**Trusted packages** field.  
Even though you can also enable the **Trust all packages** field to allow the serialization of more
classes by allowing any object to be serialized and deserialized, this is not as secure as leaving
it disabled in most cases. Keep this parameter disabled to improve the security and help prevent
malicious attacks.

In the following example, you configure the connection to enable only users who are compliant with
the `com.mulesoft.someapp` and `com.mulesoft.someapp.model` packages to consume and produce
ObjectMessages:

1.  In Studio, open the **JMS Config** global element window.
2.  In the **Connection Factory** section, set the **Factory configuration** field to **Edit
    inline**.
3.  Select the **Trusted packages** field.
4.  Click the plus sign to add a new package.
5.  In the **Trusted package** window, set the **Value** field to `com.mulesoft.someapp`.
6.  Click **Finish**.
7.  Repeat Step 4.
8.  In the **Trusted package** window, set the **Value** field to `com.mulesoft.someapp.model`.
9.  Click **Finish**.
10. Select the **Trust all packages** field.
11. Click **OK**.

![Select the Trusted packages and Trust all packages field](_images/jms-activemq-packages.png)

Figure 4. JMS Connector Trusted Packages configuration

In the XML editor, the `<jms:trusted-packages>` and `trustAllPackages` configurations look like
this:

```xml
<jms:config name="JMS_Config">
  <jms:active-mq-connection>
    <jms:factory-configuration trustAllPackages="true">
      <jms:trusted-packages >
        <jms:trusted-package value="com.mulesoft.someapp" />
        <jms:trusted-package value="com.mulesoft.someapp.model" />
      </jms:trusted-packages>
    </jms:factory-configuration>
  </jms:active-mq-connection>
</jms:config>
```

## Configure SSL Connections

JMS Connector version 1.3.0 and later enables you to configure ActiveMQ connections with SSL
configurations to establish secure and encrypted connections against the ActiveMQ broker:

1.  In Studio, navigate to the **Global Elements** tab.
2.  Click **Create**.
3.  In the filter box type `jms` and select **JMS Config**.
4.  Click **OK**.
5.  In the **Connection** field select **ActiveMQ Connection**.
6.  Navigate to the **TLS/SSL** tab.
7.  In the **TLS Configuration** field, select **Edit Inline**
8.  In the **Trust Store Configuration** section, set the following fields:
    - **Path**: `client.ts`
    - **Password**: `password`

9.  In the **Key Store Configuration** section, set the following fields:
    - **Path**: `client.ks`
    - **Alias**: `client`
    - **Key Password**: `password`
    - **Password**: `password`

10. Click **OK**.

![SSL Configuration.](_images/jms-activemq-ssl.png)

Figure 5. JMS Connector SSK Configuration

In the XML editor, the `<jms:active-mq-connection>`, `<tls:trust-store>`, and `<tls:key-store>`
configuration looks like this:

```xml
<jms:config name="JMS_Config">
  <jms:active-mq-connection>
   <tls:context>
    <tls:trust-store
      path="client.ts"
      password="password" />
    <tls:key-store
      path="client.ks"
      password="password"
      keyPassword="password"
      alias="client" />
   </tls:context>
  </jms:active-mq-connection>
</jms:config>
```

JMS Connector can also reference global TLS context configurations to reuse and share the same TLS
context between connectors as shown in the following example with Anypoint Connector for HTTP (HTTP
Connector):

```xml
<!--  HTTP Requester Configuration -->
<http:request-config name="HTTP_Request_configuration">
  <http:request-connection tlsContext="TLS_Context" />
</http:request-config>

<!--  JMS Configuration -->
<jms:config name="JMS_Config">
  <jms:active-mq-connection tlsContext="TLS_Context"/>
</jms:config>

<!--  Reusable TLS Context -->
<tls:context name="TLS_Context">
 <tls:trust-store
  path="client.ts"
  password="password" />
<tls:key-store
  path="client.ks"
  password="password"
  keyPassword="password"
  alias="client" />
</tls:context>
```

## See Also

- [JMS Connector Reference](jms-connector-reference)
---

# JMS Connector Reference 1.10

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for JMS (JMS Connector) is a JMS 1.0.2b, 1.1, and 2.0 compliant MuleSoft
Extension, used to consume and produce JMS messages. The extension supports all JMS functionality
including topics and queues, durable subscribers, acknowledgment modes and local transactions.

Release Notes: [JMS Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/connector-jms)

## Configurations

---

### Default Configuration

Base configuration for JMS Connector

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The default encoding of the message body to use if the message doesnât communicate it</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The default contentType of the message body to use if the message doesnât communicate it</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>/</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Correlation Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to specify a correlationId when publishing messages. This applies both for custom correlation IDs specifies at the operation level and for default correlation IDs taken from the current event</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ack Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p></li><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>MANUAL</p></li><li><p>DUPS_OK</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ConsumerAckMode to use when consuming a message. Can be overridden at the message source level. This attribute has to be IMMEDIATE if transactionType is LOCAL or MULTI</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>queue-consumer</p></li><li><p><a href="#topic-consumer">Topic Consumer</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ConsumerType to use by default when consuming a message. Can be overridden at the message source level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Selector</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default selector to use for filtering when consuming a message. Can be overridden at the message source level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Do not configure this parameter here, as it has no effect. See [ActiveMQConnectionFactoryConfiguration]</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Persistent Delivery</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the default value for the Message#getJMSDeliveryMode. If true, the DeliveryMode#PERSISTENT mode will be used, which instructs the JMS provider to take extra care to insure the message is not lost in transit due to a JMS provider failure.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Priority</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the default Message#getJMSPriority to use when sending messages</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>4</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the default time the message is in the broker before it expires and is discarded</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A TimeUnit which qualifies the #timeToLive attribute. Defaults to MILLISECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MILLISECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Message Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the default value to use when producing messages, for disabling the Message#getJMSMessageID generation in the broker. Depending on the provider it may or may not have effect</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Message Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the default value to use, when producing messages, for disable Message#getJMSTimestamp generation in the broker. Depending on the provider it may or may not have effect.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delivery Delay</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is used to determine the message delivery delay time which is calculated by adding the deliveryDelay value specified on the send method to the time the message was sent. Only used in JmsSpecification#JMS_2_0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delivery Delay Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A TimeUnit which qualifies the #deliveryDelay attribute. Defaults to MILLISECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MILLISECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jms Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A message JMSType identifier supplied by a client when a message is sent.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

- [Ack](#ack)
- [Consume](#consume)
- [Publish](#publish)
- [Publish Consume](#publishConsume)
- [Recover Session](#recoverSession)

### Associated Sources

- [On New Message](#listener)

### Consume

`<jms:consume>`

Operation that allows the user to consume a single message from a given Destination.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the Destination from where the message should be consumed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>queue-consumer</p></li><li><p><a href="#topic-consumer">Topic Consumer</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of the MessageConsumer that is required for the given destination, along with any extra configurations that are required based on the destination type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ack Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p></li><li><p>MANUAL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ConsumerAckMode to configure over the message and Session</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Selector</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A custom JMS selector for filtering the messages</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Messageâs content type</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Messageâs content encoding</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum Wait</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum time to wait for a message before timing out</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>10000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum Wait Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use in the maximumWaitTime configurations</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MILLISECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of joining action that operations can take regarding transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JMSAttributes">JMS Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- JMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- JMS:ACK
- JMS:DESTINATION_NOT_FOUND
- JMS:SECURITY
- JMS:TIMEOUT
- JMS:CONNECTIVITY
- JMS:CONSUMING

### Publish

`<jms:publish>`

Operation that allows the user to send a message to a JMS Destination.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the Destination where the message should be sent</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>QUEUE</p></li><li><p>TOPIC</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The DestinationType of the destination</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>QUEUE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Transactional Action for the operation. Indicates if the publish must be executed or not in a transaction.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Correlation Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>options on whether to include an outbound correlation ID or not</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The body of the message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>JMS Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSType header of the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Correlation ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSCorrelationID header of the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Content-Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>True if the body type should be sent as a message property</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content-Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The content type of the body</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>True if the body outboundEncoding should be sent as a message property</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The outboundEncoding of the messageâs body</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JmsDestination">JMS Destination</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSReplyTo header information of the Destination where this message should be replied to</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The custom user properties that should be set to this message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>JMSX Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JmsxProperties">JMSX Properties</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSX properties that should be set to this message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Persistent Delivery</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, the message is sent using the PERSISTENT JMSDeliveryMode</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Priority</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The default JMSPriority value to use when sending the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the default time the message is in the broker before it expires and is discarded</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use in the timeToLive configurations</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Message Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, the message is flagged to avoid generating its MessageID</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Message Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, the message is flagged to avoid generating its sent Timestamp</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delivery Delay</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only used by JMS 2.0. Sets the delivery delay to be applied to postpone the message delivery</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delivery Delay Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use in the deliveryDelay configurations</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- JMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- JMS:ILLEGAL_BODY
- JMS:PUBLISHING
- JMS:DESTINATION_NOT_FOUND
- JMS:SECURITY
- JMS:CONNECTIVITY

### Publish Consume

`<jms:publish-consume>`

Operation that allows the user to send a message to a JMS Destination and waits for a response
either to the provided ReplyTo destination or to a temporary Destination created dynamically.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the Destination where the message should be sent</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request-Reply Pattern</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CORRELATION_ID</p></li><li><p>MESSAGE_ID</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>CORRELATION_ID</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Correlation ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>options on whether to include an outbound correlation ID or not</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The body of the message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>JMS Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSType header of the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Correlation ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSCorrelationID header of the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Content-Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>True if the body type should be sent as a message property</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content-Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The content type of the body</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>True if the body outboundEncoding should be sent as a message property</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The outboundEncoding of the messageâs body</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JmsDestination">JMS Destination</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSReplyTo header information of the Destination where this message should be replied to</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The custom user properties that should be set to this message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>JMSX Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JmsxProperties">JMSX Properties</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSX properties that should be set to this message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Persistent Delivery</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, the message is sent using the PERSISTENT JMSDeliveryMode</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Priority</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The default JMSPriority value to use when sending the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the default time the message is in the broker before it expires and is discarded</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use in the timeToLive configurations</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Message Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, the message is flagged to avoid generating its MessageID</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Message Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, the message is flagged to avoid generating its sent Timestamp</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delivery Delay</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only used by JMS 2.0. Sets the delivery delay to be applied to postpone the message delivery</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delivery Delay Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use in the deliveryDelay configurations</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ack Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p></li><li><p>MANUAL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Session ACK mode to use when consuming the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum Wait</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum time to wait for a message to arrive before timeout</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>10000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum Wait Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use in the maximumWaitTime configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MILLISECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The content type of the message body to be consumed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The encoding of the message body to be consumed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JMSAttributes">JMS Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- JMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- JMS:ILLEGAL_BODY
- JMS:ACK
- JMS:PUBLISHING
- JMS:DESTINATION_NOT_FOUND
- JMS:SECURITY
- JMS:TIMEOUT
- JMS:CONNECTIVITY
- JMS:CONSUMING

### Ack

`<jms:ack>`

Allows the user to perform an ACK when the AckMode#MANUAL mode is elected while consuming the
Message. As per JMS Spec, performing an ACK over a single message automatically works as an ACK for
all the Messages produced in the same JmsSession.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Ack Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The AckId of the message to ACK</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Throws

- JMS:ACK

### Recover Session

`<jms:recover-session>`

Allows the user to perform a session recover when the AckMode#MANUAL mode is elected while consuming
the Message. As per JMS Spec, performing a session recover automatically redelivers all the consumed
messages that had not being acknowledged before this recover.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Ack Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The AckId of the message Session to recover</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Throws

- JMS:SESSION_RECOVER

## Sources

### On New Message

`<jms:listener>`

JMS Subscriber for Destinations, allows to listen for incoming Messages

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the Destination from where the message should be consumed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>queue-consumer</p></li><li><p><a href="#topic-consumer">Topic Consumer</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Type of the Consumer that should be used for the provided destination</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Acknowledge Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p></li><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>MANUAL</p></li><li><p>DUPS_OK</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Session ACK mode to use when consuming a message. The acknowledgment mode is determined as follows:<br></p></div><div><ul><li><p>If the listener has ackMode="MANUAL", and the Config has ackMode="AUTO", then the listener uses ackMode="MANUAL"<br></p></li><li><p>If the listener has ackMode=null, and the Config has ackMode="MANUAL", then the listener uses ackMode="MANUAL"<br></p></li><li><p>If the listener has ackMode=null, and the Config has ackMode=null, then the listener uses ackMode="AUTO" (the consumer config default)</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Selector</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JMS selector to use for filtering incoming messages</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Inbound Content-Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The content type of the message body</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Inbound Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The inboundEncoding of the message body</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Number Of Consumers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of concurrent consumers to use to receive JMS Messages</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>4</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_BEGIN</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of beginning action that sources can take regarding transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NONE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transaction Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>LOCAL</p></li><li><p>XA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of transaction to create. Availability depends on Mule version.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>LOCAL</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in Cluster</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ignore JMS replyTo header</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>True if no automatic response is needed for messages with destination header</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The body of the message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>JMS Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSType header of the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Correlation ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSCorrelationID header of the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Content-Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>True if the body type should be sent as a message property</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content-Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The content type of the body</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>True if the body outboundEncoding should be sent as a message property</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The outboundEncoding of the messageâs body</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JmsDestination">JMS Destination</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSReplyTo header information of the Destination where this message should be replied to</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The custom user properties that should be set to this message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>JMSX Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JmsxProperties">JMSX Properties</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The JMSX properties that should be set to this message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Persistent Delivery</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not the delivery should be done with a persistent configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Priority</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The default JMSPriority value to use when sending the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the default time the message is in the broker before it expires and is discarded</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use in the timeToLive configurations</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Message ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, the message is flagged to avoid generating its MessageID</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disable Message Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, the message is flagged to avoid generating its sent Timestamp</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delivery Delay</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only used by JMS 2.0. Sets the delivery delay to be applied to postpone the message delivery</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delivery Delay Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use in the deliveryDelay configurations</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Correlation ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Options on whether to include an outbound correlation ID or not</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Reply Pattern</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CORRELATION_ID</p></li><li><p>MESSAGE_ID</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates which Request Reply Pattern to use. By default uses the Correlation ID of the incoming message to do the Reply-To. In case of configuring the message ID pattern, the ReplyTo will be performed using the message ID of the incoming message. If NONE is selected, not correlation ID will be configured automatically.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>CORRELATION_ID</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JMSAttributes">JMS Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

## Types

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### JMS Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JMSMessageProperties">JMS Message Properties</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Container element for all the properties present in a JMS Message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>All the possible headers of a JMS message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ack Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The session Ack ID required to acknowledge the current message if one is available, otherwise, it is null.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### JMS Message Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>All</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>All the properties of the JMS message as a flattened map.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user-provided properties of the JMS message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jms Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JMS message broker and provider properties.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JMSXProperties">JMSX Properties</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JMSX properties of the JMS message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### JMSX Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx User ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user identity that sends the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx App ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Identity of the application that sends the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Delivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of message delivery attempts.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Group ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The message group identity of the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Group Seq</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The sequence number of the message in the group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Producer TXID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Identifier of the transaction that produced the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Consumer TXID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Identifier of the transaction that consumed the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Rcv Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The time JMS delivered the message to the consumer.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Do not configure this parameter here, as it has no effect. See [ActiveMQConnectionFactoryConfiguration]</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secure hashing algorithm to use. If not set, the default is SHA-256.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property may only be set if useSecureHash is false.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message is stored.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to reconnect (in milliseconds)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of reconnection attempts to make</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### JMS Destination

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Destination</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name that identifies the destination where to send a reply to a message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>QUEUE</p></li><li><p>TOPIC</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>the type of this destination</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>QUEUE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### JMSX Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx User ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The user identity that sends the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx App ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Identity of the application that sends the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Delivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of message delivery attempts.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Group ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The message group identity of the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Group Seq</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The sequence number of the message in the group.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Producer TXID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Identifier of the transaction that produced the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Consumer TXID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Identifier of the transaction that consumed the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Jmsx Rcv Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The time JMS delivered the message to the consumer.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#standard-revocation-check">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#custom-ocsp-responder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#crl-file">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store
